business.headline: Atanu Chakraborty: Retired IAS officer whose abrupt exit from HDFC has raised eyebrowsIn his resignation letter, Atanu Chakraborty flagged concerns over the bank's internal functioning, stating that "certain happenings and practices within the bank" over the past two years were "not in congruence with my personal values and ethics".

HDFC Bank shares tumble 5%. Here's why the stock is down todayThe HDFC Bank stock emerged among the top losers on the benchmark indices, underperforming the broader market amid concerns around governance after Chakraborty stepped down citing issues related to "values and ethics".

Sensex down 1,800 points: What's behind today's stock market crash?The Sensex plunged over 1,800 points and the Nifty fell more than 2% as surging crude oil prices and HDFC Bank concerns triggered a broad-based sell-off across sectors.

Live: 2 dead, 9 injured in Surat LPG cylinder blast during illegal refillingLPG crisis live updates: The government has flagged that the LPG supply situation continues to be under pressure and has urged consumers to use cooking gas carefully. At the same time, efforts are underway to expand piped natural gas networks to reduce reliance on LPG. In a major nationwide crackdown, officials said over 12,000 raids have been conducted and more than 15,000 cylinders seized to curb hoarding and black marketing under the Essential Commodities Act. Authorities are also encouraging commercial users in cities to shift to PNG, with simplified connections and incentives being offered. Online LPG bookings have climbed to around 94%, while commercial supplies are gradually resuming in several states with government support. Despite the arrival of fresh shipments like Shivalik and Nanda Devi, the effects of earlier disruptions are still being seen across sectors. Follow this live blog for the latest updates on LPG supply, government action and the situation across cities and industries in India.

Sensex, Nifty fall as crude oil surge rattles markets amid Middle East tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex plummeted 1,446.01 points to 75,258.12, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 441.45 points to 23,336.35 as of 9:28 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock market likely to crash today. Here's whyEarly signals from GIFT Nifty suggest a weak start. As of 8:30 am, GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,284, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open well below Wednesday's closing level of 23,777.8.

HDFC Bank shares to be in focus today. Here's whyGlobal brokerage JPMorgan has maintained a "neutral" rating on the stock with a price target of Rs 1,090. HDFC Bank stock is already trading near its 52-week low, adding to weak sentiment around the counter.

Atanu Chakraborty resigns as HDFC Bank part-time chairman over ethics issuesHDFC Bank part-time chairman Atanu Chakraborty resigned citing differences over values and ethics, with the RBI approving Keki Mistry as interim part-time chairman for a three-month period.

US Fed holds rates, flags higher inflation risks amid US-Iran war tensionsFederal Reserve kept rates unchanged, signaled one possible cut in 2026, and projected higher inflation amid Middle East tensions, while markets reacted cautiously and unemployment outlook remained steady.

Reliance Jio hires banks for IPO, no new capital to be raised: ReportThe IPO is expected to be structured as an "offer for sale" (OFS). This means existing investors will sell a part of their stake to the public, rather than the company issuing new shares.

Hidden reasons why life insurance claims get delayed or rejectedFiling a life insurance claim should be a straightforward process, especially during difficult times. Yet, many families face unexpected delays or rejections, often due to small details they overlooked. What really goes wrong?

Russian oil tanker bound for China diverts to India's New Mangalore PortA Russian oil tanker, Aqua Titan, originally bound for China, has diverted course in the South China Sea and is now headed to India's New Mangalore port, set to arrive on March 21st.

7 China-bound Russian oil tankers make U-turn, head to IndiaThe rerouting of these vessels carrying Russian oil comes after India received a temporary waiver from the US to buy 'sanctioned' Russian oil currently stranded at sea.

Iran-US war impact may push up medicine prices in IndiaPharma industry bodies have already raised the issue with the government, sending an emergency report highlighting the risk to raw material supplies.

Why borrowing now feels safer than saving for many IndiansToday, a growing number of Indians—especially younger, urban consumers—are embracing a very different model: spend first, borrow easily, and worry about repayment later.
Sensex ends 633 points higher, Nifty above 23,700; Infosys up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 633.29 points to close at 76,704.13, while the NSE Nifty50 added 196.65 points to end at 23,777.80.

Fixed deposits are boring, but are they the smartest bet right now?Fixed deposits may have fallen out of favour in recent years, but they never really disappeared. Even as investors chased higher returns in equities and gold, a large share of Indian savings stayed parked in banks.

Delhi High Court dismisses Peru's plea for sole rights over 'PISCO' nameThe case goes back to 2005, when Peru applied for GI protection for 'PISCO' in India. Chile opposed the move, claiming it had long used the name for its own grape-based spirit.

Nithin Kamath criticises banking apps for seeking unnecessary permissionsKamath referred to the Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP), which means apps should only ask for the minimum access required to function.

Why gold isn't rising despite Iran-US war and what it means for your moneyWar and uncertainty typically push gold prices higher, but the current trend tells a different story. Despite rising geopolitical tensions, gold is moving in a narrow range; why isn't it surging this time?

entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 breaks all premiere records with Rs 52 crore, set for historic run Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has begun its box office journey with record-breaking paid premiere collections exceeding Rs 52 crore.  The film is expected to dominate the box office with positive reviews from preview shows.

With no loud drama, it taps into the confusion and comfort of modern relationships, with Santosh Sobhan and Manasa Varanasi in lead roles.

Reality show fame Josh Duggar's brother arrested for allegedly molesting 9-year-old Joseph Duggar, brother of Josh Duggar, has been arrested in Florida on charges involving sexual activity with a minor. Details of the investigation and allegations are emerging.

The controversial song from the upcoming pan-Indian Kannada film KD - The Devil has drawn criticism for its reportedly vulgar lyrics. While the Hindi version drew maximum backlash, the debate over whether the original Kannada version was equally suggestive has been gaining momentum.

By eliminating consensus-builder Larijani, the Israelis might have removed any scope of talks and pushed Iran into deeper hardline control. This could be a ploy to keep the war going.

Live: Qatar brings down fire at Ras Laffan LNG hub hit by Iranian missilesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The United States was informed in advance of Israel's strike on Iran's South Pars gas field but did not take part, as global oil prices surged on fears of retaliation. Hours later, a missile hit Qatar's key gas facility, causing significant damage and prompting Doha to expel some Iranian embassy officials. Israel also killed Iran's intelligence minister, further destabilising its leadership, as Tehran warned of retaliation. Meanwhile, an Iranian missile struck a building in the occupied West Bank, killing three people and injuring at least 13 — the first fatalities there since the Iran war began. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

The sandwich generation layoff: When parents lose jobs and children start earningAs layoffs hit professionals in their early 50s, Indian households are witnessing an unexpected shift. Pakistan among biggest nuclear threats to US, says Intel chief Tulsi GabbardGabbard said that the U.S. intelligence community assesses a sharp rise in the number of missiles capable of striking the United States. According to estimates, the figure is expected to grow to over 16,000 by 2035, a significant increase from the current assessed number of around 3,000.
